big_picture: The government of India has asked states to stay alert on supplies and prices amid the West Asia conflict. Prime Minister Modi made a second call to Saudi Crown Prince to discuss the conflict. The West Asia conflict is shaping upcoming state election narratives, with potential impacts on India's FY27 GDP outlook. The RBI has extended export incentives to help mitigate the effects of the conflict. The government has proposed amendments to the Foreign Contribution Regulation Act, which is reshaping the political narrative in Central Travancore ahead of the Kerala Assembly poll 2026. The Kerala High Court has summoned the Law Secretary to explain the delay in tabling the Kerala Prohibition of Ragging (Amendment) Bill, 2025 before the State Cabinet. Bengali migrants in New Delhi are rushing home amid voter list fears ahead of West Bengal polls, with several migrant workers reporting deletions or discrepancies in the electoral rolls following the revision exercise. The Centre has proposed an amendment to the IT rules to allow blocking of independent news creators, which advocacy group Internet Freedom Foundation has called a "massive expansion of unconstitutional censorship and regulatory power."

story_clusters.headline: How West Asia conflict is shaping upcoming state election narratives
story_clusters.summary: Prime Minister Modi has asked states to ensure supply stability and curb hoarding amid the West Asia crisis. He made this request for the second time since the conflict began, with the latest call being with the Saudi Crown Prince. The ongoing conflict in West Asia is also expected to potentially reduce India's GDP growth by 1 percentage point in FY27.

Trade.summary:
The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) has removed the ₹10 lakh cap on courier exports to boost e-commerce trade. This move aims to facilitate the growth of e-commerce trade in the country.

The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has extended the export credit window till June 30 due to disruptions in West Asia. This decision is intended to provide relief to exporters affected by the disruptions.

The RBI has also extended the export realisation timeline amid global disruptions, providing trade relief to exporters.

articles.title: ECI Not Targetting Bengal, More Officers Transferred In Other Poll-Bound States: Calcutta High Court

articles.summary: The Madras High Court recently observed that unless there was a dedication or declaration of property for public charitable purpose, mere usage of the words “Waqf” or “Mosque” in a settlement deed would not mean that a property has been declared as public waqf. Justice PB Balaji observed, “At the risk of repetition, mere use of the words ' Waqf ' and 'Mosque' will not imply that there has been creation of a public waqf under the deed,”. articles.title: 'Matter Of Grave Concern': MP High Court Flags Rise Of Illegal Sand Transport In Overloaded Vehicles Threatening Public Safety

articles.title: Can Women Above 50-Years Of Age Be Permitted To Avail Assisted Reproductive Technology Services? Bombay High Court To Decide
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court is set to decide whether women above 50-years of age can be held to be 'medically fit' to bear a child and give birth with the help of Assisted Reproductive Techniques (ART). A division bench of Justice Ravindra Ghuge and Justice Abhay Mantri heard two petitions filed by women - a 55-year-old and a 53-year-old - both challenging the validity of the Section 21 (g) of the Assisted Reproductive Technology Act, 2021. The said provisions mandates a strict age limit for both men

The event commenced with registration at 09:00 AM

articles.title: Telangana relied on RBI borrowings for most of FY25: CAG report
articles.summary: Telangana's finances in 2024-25 saw extensive use of RBI's Special Drawing Facility and Ways and Means Advances. The state government borrowed Rs 27,730 crore via SDF and Rs 64,188 crore through WMA. Overdrafts totaling Rs 37,457 crore were also availed. This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

This reliance on short-term borrowing mechanisms highlights financial management challenges. The CAG report detailed these transactions.

articles.title: Identity on trial: Why India's new transgender law is facing backlash
articles.summary: India's new Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026, has sparked widespread protests by removing self-identification rights. Critics argue the law replaces individual autonomy with mandatory medical certification, contradicting previous legal precedents and the Supreme Court's NALSA judgment. Activists fear this regressive move will hinder access to essential rights and services for the transgender community.

articles.title: Services, remittances boost India's invisible inflows to $464 billion in FY26
articles.summary: Receipts from the services sector, which accounted for two-third of the total, climbed about 9% to $310 billion, data showed. These are known as invisible receipts as these reflect earnings a country generates from IT services, tourism and remittances   transfers rather than international trade in physical goods.

articles.title: 'India's economy will double every 7-8 years,' says Union Minister Gajendra Singh Shekhawat
articles.summary: India's economy is set for significant growth, aiming for 30 trillion dollars by 2047. However, ongoing global conflicts pose risks. These tensions could reduce India's economic expansion by one percent and increase inflation by 1.5 percent. The nation's reliance on imported energy makes it vulnerable to these disruptions. Despite these challenges, India's economy shows strong momentum.

articles.title: Iran war shock: Middle East conflict could cut 1 ppt from India’s FY27 GDP outlook
articles.summary: Iran War: A persistent Middle East conflict could reduce India's real GDP growth by 1 percentage point and increase retail inflation by 1.5 percentage points from baseline estimates. This impact stems from disruptions to global oil and energy markets, affecting employment-intensive sectors and aggregate demand. The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

The government may need to implement countercyclical policies and augment the Economic Stabilization Fund.

articles.title: Sebi cracks down on Elitecon International, bars promoters from market for manipulation
articles.summary: Sebi passed an interim order against Elitecon International and barred key promoters over alleged misleading disclosures, governance lapses and suspicious trading. articles.title: India included: Scaling access, enabling growth
articles.summary: Until recently, large sections of India operated outside the formal financial system. Banking infrastructure struggled to keep pace with the country’s scale and diversity. For a long time, branch banking was the only option; and that limited access.”

articles.title: India diesel exports jump 20% in March amid Iran war
articles.summary: India boosted diesel exports significantly in March. This surge was driven by favorable economics for middle distillates. Geopolitical events in West Asia tightened supply, increasing diesel and jet fuel prices. Refiners adjusted production to capitalize on these higher margins. India also increased domestic LPG production to compensate for lost imports. Overall refined product exports saw a slight decline.

articles.title: Supervisory Data Quality Index for Scheduled Commercial Banks  (December 2025)
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has released Supervisory Data Quality Index (sDQI) for SCBs for December 2025 . It measures data quality in terms of the Accuracy, Timeliness, Completeness and Consistency in the submission of returns. The objective of sDQI is to assess the adherence to the principles enunciated in the Master Direction on Filing of Supervisory Returns 2024 . (Brij Raj)             Chief General Manager Press Release: 2025-2026/2361

articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India had issued the final Amendment Directions on Capital Market Exposure on February 13, 2026 , after due consideration of the feedback received as part of public consultation. articles.summary: The Himachal Pradesh High Court held that the question of cheque dishonour cannot be conclusively determined merely based on a return memo. articles.title: No Second Bite at the Cherry: SC Bars Execution After Abandoning Title Challenge
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India held that parties cannot enforce rights in execution after abandoning earlier suits on the same issue. The key takeaway is that non-prosecution of prior claims can bar relief even if res judicata does not apply. articles.title: India proposes making government advisories legally binding on tech giants
articles.summary: India is proposing new rules for internet platforms. Advisories and clarifications from the IT ministry will now be legally binding. Public feedback is invited until April 14.

articles.title: India approves over $750 million in projects for electronic component manufacturing
articles.summary: India has approved 29 electronics manufacturing proposals. This initiative involves an investment of over 71 billion rupees. This move is set to expand India's electronics manufacturing capacity significantly. The goal is to reach 500 billion dollars in production by fiscal 2031.

This initiative follows the recent arrest and subsequent bail of its founders in connection with an alleged investment scam. The funds will enhance cybersecurity, launch an AI-powered helpline, and support law enforcement training nationwide.

The funds will enhance cybersecurity, launch an AI-powered helpline, and support law enforcement training nationwide.

articles.title: West Asia crisis: Government in talks with tech industry associations; no adverse impact reported yet
articles.summary: The government is in talks with hi-tech industry bodies over the West Asia conflict, with no adverse impact reported so far, Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw said. Despite concerns around a helium crunch affecting supply chains, sectors like semiconductors and electronics remain stable, though the situation is still evolving, according to PTI.

Despite concerns around a helium crunch affecting supply chains, sectors like semiconductors and electronics remain stable, though the situation is still evolving, according to PTI.

articles.title: "Unfortunate for India," Ex R&AW Chief hints at probable inflation if West Asia situation escalates
articles.summary: Former R AW Chief Vikram Sood expressed concern over the West Asia conflict's impact on India, particularly potential oil and fertilizer shortages leading to inflation. He called the attack on Iran and the killing of its Supreme Leader unfortunate, emphasizing India's dependence on the Strait of Hormuz while noting Israel as an ally.

He called the attack on Iran and the killing of its Supreme Leader unfortunate, emphasizing India's dependence on the Strait of Hormuz while noting Israel as an ally.

articles.title: Pulses import duties likely to stay unchanged
articles.summary: New Delhi is set to maintain current import duties on pulses. The government will also extend the duty-free period for tur and urad. This move aims to prevent price increases. Officials anticipate an El Nino affecting monsoon rainfall. Production of tur and urad is expected to decline. This could lead to higher prices and food inflation.

Officials anticipate an El Nino affecting monsoon rainfall. Production of tur and urad is expected to decline. This could lead to higher prices and food inflation.

articles.title: Fiscal deficit until February eases to 80.4% of FY26 target
articles.summary: In absolute terms, the fiscal deficit between April 2025 and February 2026 stood at ₹12.53 lakh crore against ₹13.47 lakh crore a year ago. The government has pegged the 2025-26 fiscal deficit at ₹15.58 lakh crore.

articles.title: RBI should use forex reserves to prop up rupee: SBI report
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India should use its substantial foreign exchange reserves to stabilize the Indian Rupee. A report from SBI suggests this action is necessary as the ongoing West Asia crisis impacts global markets. The report highlights that India possesses ample reserves to manage speculative moves.

A report from SBI suggests this action is necessary as the ongoing West Asia crisis impacts global markets. The report highlights that India possesses ample reserves to manage speculative moves.

articles.title: Road ministry awards 2257 km roads; constructs 4,989 km until December 31, 2025: Annual Report 2025-26
articles.summary: This reflects a considerable slowdown in the pace of awarding and construction of national highways in the country which could impact the targets set for 2025-26 when the full year data for FY’26 is available. As per the report, the ministry has already utilized 83.39% or Rs 2.27 lakh crore of the total budgetary capital outlay of Rs 2.72 lakh crore for 2025-26 till December 31, 2025.

articles.title: India's net international liability comes down by $10.9 billion in the third quarter
articles.summary: India's financial obligations to the world decreased by $10.9 billion in the third fiscal quarter. This improvement was driven by Indian residents increasing their overseas assets more than foreigners increased their assets in India. The ratio of India's international assets to liabilities saw a positive shift. Indian residents invested more abroad in direct investments and deposits.

This improvement was driven by Indian residents increasing their overseas assets more than foreigners increased their assets in India. The ratio of India's international assets to liabilities saw a positive shift. Indian residents invested more abroad in direct investments and deposits.

articles.title: Sales of pvt non-financial public cos rise 7.8% in FY25: RBI data
articles.summary: Select NGNF public limited companies experienced a rise in net sales growth to 7.8 percent in 2024-25. Manufacturing and services sectors both saw increased sales. Operating expenses also grew, impacting operating profits. However, profit after tax surged significantly due to non-operating income and lower tax expenses.

Operating expenses also grew, impacting operating profits. However, profit after tax surged significantly due to non-operating income and lower tax expenses.

articles.title: Industrial production rises to 5.2% in February, led by manufacturing
articles.summary: India's industrial production saw a 5.2% rise in February, a slight increase from January. Manufacturing output drove this growth. Economists anticipate a slowdown in March due to global conflicts. For the fiscal year 2026, industrial growth has remained steady. Infrastructure and capital goods are key drivers of this expansion.

Infrastructure and capital goods are key drivers of this expansion.

articles.title: India’s industrial growth rises to 5.2% in February from 4.8% in January
articles.summary: February 2026 marked a pivotal moment for India's industrial sector, which saw a 5.2 percent increase in the Index of Industrial Production year-on-year. The surge can be largely attributed to dynamic manufacturing activities, while the mining and electricity industries played crucial roles in propelling overall economic momentum. The trend indicates a continued expansion in industrial undertakings.

The trend indicates a continued expansion in industrial undertakings.

articles.title: Every $10 rise in crude may add 60 bps to India's inflation: CareEdge Global
articles.summary: Rising crude oil prices due to West Asian tensions could significantly push up India's inflation by 55-60 basis points per $10 barrel increase in FY27. This could also widen the current account deficit and pressure the Indian rupee, though strong domestic demand and policy flexibility offer some resilience.
articles.raw_summary: Rising crude oil prices due to West Asian tensions could significantly push up India's inflation by 55-60 basis points per $10 barrel increase in FY27. This could also widen the current account deficit and pressure the Indian rupee, though strong domestic demand and policy flexibility offer some resilience.

articles.title: Government to likely halt disbursement under ECMS if design, Six Sigma quality not met: Ashwini Vaishnaw
articles.summary: The government is set to review its electronics manufacturing scheme. Beneficiaries must invest in product design and achieve Six Sigma standards. Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw warned that non-compliance will lead to halted disbursements and approvals. The industry has 15 days to present plans for design, talent, and local sourcing. This move aims to boost high-quality, self-reliant electronics manufacturing in India.

Union Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw warned that non-compliance will lead to halted disbursements and approvals. The industry has 15 days to present plans for design, talent, and local sourcing. This move aims to boost high-quality, self-reliant electronics manufacturing in India.

articles.title: Delhi’s factory count rises but jobs lag, signalling shift in industrial growth
articles.summary: Delhi's factories have increased, but job growth in its industrial sector remains slow. This indicates a gradual recovery after the Covid-19 pandemic. Employment gains are concentrated in specific segments like rubber, plastics, and repair services. Traditional sectors like textiles show stagnation. A shift towards logistics and supply chain activities is also evident.

A shift towards logistics and supply chain activities is also evident.

articles.title: Israel, Iran war: ‘Situation very vulnerable,' Urea output impacted, India govt official says
articles.summary: Israel, Iran war: India faces a vulnerable situation with domestic urea production impacted. The government is actively securing imports for April and May, crucial months for agricultural stocking. States are being informed to prevent panic. Authorities are vigilant against hoarding and black marketing. Promotion of alternative fertilizers like ammonium sulphate and triple super phosphate is also underway to manage urea demand.

The government is actively securing imports for April and May, crucial months for agricultural stocking. States are being informed to prevent panic. Authorities are vigilant against hoarding and black marketing. Promotion of alternative fertilizers like ammonium sulphate and triple super phosphate is also underway to manage urea demand.

articles.title: War fades the Goldilocks moment. RBI's next move will matter
articles.summary: India's brief 'Goldilocks' period of high growth and low inflation is over, challenged by global events like the Iran war. Rising oil prices and supply chain disruptions are creating a classic dilemma for the RBI, forcing a choice between supporting growth and containing inflation amidst growing uncertainty.

Rising oil prices and supply chain disruptions are creating a classic dilemma for the RBI, forcing a choice between supporting growth and containing inflation amidst growing uncertainty.

articles.title: Australia seeks 'another crack' at trade deal with India in coming weeks
articles.summary: India and Australia are set to resume trade negotiations in the coming weeks, with Canberra eager to advance a bilateral agreement. Discussions are expected to progress gradually through a phased approach, focusing on India's growing consumer market and demand for Australian food and beverages. While both nations are optimistic, negotiations are anticipated to be complex and unfold over multiple stages.

While both nations are optimistic, negotiations are anticipated to be complex and unfold over multiple stages.

articles.title: Strong domestic demand supports India's economy, but Middle East crisis raises stagflation risks: Morgan Stanley
articles.summary: India's economic outlook is bolstered by robust domestic demand and improving high-frequency indicators, including strong auto sales and GST collections. However, rising geopolitical tensions, particularly in the Middle East, present significant stagflation risks and could impact growth and macro stability. External vulnerabilities remain a concern due to reliance on Middle Eastern energy and its importance for Indian exports and remittances.

articles.title: What changes will RBI’s new rules bring to digital payments from April 1?
articles.summary: India's digital payments are getting a security upgrade. From April 1, the Reserve Bank of India will require two-factor authentication for all digital transactions. This move aims to combat rising fraud risks. The new rules will incorporate dynamic elements like OTPs or biometrics. This strengthens India's payment system and aligns it with global security standards.

This move aims to combat rising fraud risks. The new rules will incorporate dynamic elements like OTPs or biometrics. This strengthens India's payment system and aligns it with global security standards.

articles.title: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to visit Canada in May for trade talks
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal will visit Canada in May 2026. This visit coincides with ongoing talks for a free trade agreement between India and Canada. Discussions will focus on expediting these negotiations. Both nations will explore cooperation in clean energy, nuclear energy, agriculture, and critical minerals. The minister also met with UK and EU officials regarding trade pacts.

The minister also met with UK and EU officials regarding trade pacts.

articles.title: India proposes “new approaches” for farm talks at WTO consistent with Doha mandate
articles.summary: India urged the WTO to prioritize the development dimension of the Doha Work Programme, emphasizing mandated issues like Public Stockholding and Special Safeguard Mechanism. New Delhi stressed these should be addressed independently, not contingent on other reforms, and called for strengthening special and differential treatment for developing nations.

articles.title: Govt may unveil more relief packages for MSMEs, vulnerable sectors if West Asia crisis worsens
articles.summary: India's government is considering further economic relief for vulnerable sectors and MSMEs. This comes as the West Asia crisis continues, impacting global crude prices and supply chains. Measures like excise duty cuts on fuel and support for exporters are already in place. The government aims to shield the economy from rising costs and potential disruptions.

articles.title: Adequate fertiliser stock available, farmers will not face shortage despite Israel-Iran war: IFFCO
articles.summary: India's fertiliser supply remains steady despite the West Asia crisis. Production and distribution are proceeding without disruption. Senior officials confirm ample stock to meet farmers' needs for the upcoming season. The government is actively monitoring the situation and has contingency plans in place. Diversified raw material imports are also being secured to ensure continued availability.

articles.title: WTO talks near deal on reform roadmap amid US-India e-commerce deadlock
articles.summary: Global trade ministers are nearing a World Trade Organization reform agreement, with crucial talks underway in Cameroon. A key sticking point remains the extension of a moratorium on customs duties for digital downloads, a move vital for U.S. support. India's stance on a shorter extension is creating tension, potentially impacting the WTO's future relevance amidst ongoing trade disputes.

articles.title: Finance Ministry flags rising external risks as trade deficit and CAD expand
articles.summary: India's economy faces growing external pressures. Trade dynamics are weakening, with imports surging and exports declining. This widens the trade deficit significantly. Capital flows are negative due to global uncertainty. Rising crude oil prices add further strain. These factors are impacting the Indian Rupee.

articles.title: RBI's weekend move bought some relief, but no end to Re depreciation pressure, says Naveen Mathur
articles.summary: The Indian rupee experienced a brief appreciation after the RBI's intervention to curb speculative dollar buying. However, structural headwinds like the Middle East conflict, high crude oil prices, FPI outflows, and global rate hikes continue to pressure the currency. The RBI has deployed significant forex reserves, but its ability to intervene indefinitely is limited.

articles.title: LNG prices likely to hold above $18: S&P Global Energy
articles.summary: Asia’s heavy reliance on Middle East LNG supplies is emerging as a key vulnerability, warns Kenneth Foo, Global Director-LNG Pricing at S P Global Energy, with disruptions forcing countries to seek costlier alternatives and switch fuels. As supply constraints persist and replacement cargoes come at higher freight costs, energy-importing nations like India face growing pressure due to limited storage buffers and dependence on imported gas for critical sectors.
